key_drivers: Very high similarity between 93000 and 93010 (0.88) — exceeds 0.85 bundling threshold; strong procedural overlap signal
Modifier 59 applied to 93010 (ECG Interpretation Only) — evaluated against all co-billed codes on this claim

reasoning.embedding_interpretation: The dominant pair (93000 ↔ 93010) yields a cosine similarity of 0.88, placing it firmly in the >0.85 'likely duplicate or component' zone. The embedding model encodes these codes as near-identical in procedural semantics — consistent with a parent-child or component billing relationship. The residual difference (0.12) represents noise rather than clinically meaningful distinction.
reasoning.bundling_likelihood: HIGH likelihood of component bundling or duplication. One code likely subsumes the other under standard NCCI logic. Billing both separately constitutes unbundling unless extraordinary circumstances (separate sites, separate providers, separate encounters) are documented.
reasoning.limitations: IMPORTANT: This analysis is based solely on CPT embedding similarity. No diagnosis codes, anatomical site labels, provider specialty, or documentation context are available. Embedding similarity is a proxy for procedural relatedness — it cannot confirm or deny: (a) different anatomical sites, (b) separate clinical encounters, (c) distinct medical necessity for each service, or (d) payer-specific bundling policies. Uncertainty level: LOW (similarity is decisive).

axis_contributions.60-69: VERY HIGH (0.88). Near-maximum bundling cohesion detected. The dominant pair's embedding proximity strongly implies a comprehensive/component relationship. This axis is the primary driver of the high risk score.
axis_contributions.70-79: HIGH (avg similarity: 0.88). All codes cluster tightly in the same procedural family. High intra-claim procedural homogeneity — consistent with same-technique or same-system billing.
axis_contributions.80-89: HIGH. The combination of high similarity and Modifier 59 usage is a documented FWA pattern (unbundling). The embedding independently corroborates the risk without external labels. Automated denial or pre-payment review is supported.

counterfactual: Modifier 59 on the flagged code could be reconsidered ONLY if: (1) Medical records explicitly document that 93000 and 93010 were performed on anatomically distinct sites (e.g., different body regions, different laterality), (2) Separate encounters or distinct timed blocks are documented with individual procedure notes, (3) Different performing providers (separate NPIs) executed each service, or (4) A payer-specific policy explicitly permits unbundled billing for this pair. None of these conditions are detectable from embedding similarity alone. Current evidence (similarity: 0.88) supports denial or human review escalation.

axis_contributions.60-69: MODERATE (0.79). Meaningful cohesion without crossing the definitive threshold. Shared procedural logic is present but a strict parent-child relationship is not confirmed.
axis_contributions.70-79: HIGH (avg similarity: 0.79). All codes cluster tightly in the same procedural family. High intra-claim procedural homogeneity — consistent with same-technique or same-system billing.

counterfactual: Modifier 59 could be CONFIRMED VALID if: (1) Therapy/procedure notes document distinct timed units with separate clinical goals for 97110 and 97140, (2) Services were applied to anatomically distinct body regions within the same encounter, (3) Payer policy explicitly permits same-session concurrent billing for this pair with Modifier 59. Modifier 59 would be INVALIDATED if a single continuous time block was split across both codes. Recommendation: Request documentation before rendering a final determination. Similarity (0.79) alone is insufficient to decide.
